other_observers: Brenda Linfield; I was not the original finder of this bird. It had previously been reported on cobirds.
description: The bird had an obvious pelican body and bill.  The bird was substantially larger than adjacent Double-crested Cormorants.  It had a brown back and neck, with a pale belly and breast.  It had a brown cap.  It had a white face. The forehead was white, with flecks of brown.  It had dark eyes.  The bill was dark, but with a pale (dull yellowish) upper ridge.Behaviors: standing on an island with Double-crested CormorantsCall: none heardPlumage: first year
